title: pastinaca incana
intro: Pastinaca incana, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial plant in the carrot family native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pastinaca incana include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as supporting digestive health and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of wild parsnip include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the plant's root or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
